perturbation.original_text: Derivative Materials will be immediately destroyed at the request of the Company and such destruction will be confirmed to the Company in writing by an officer of the Recipient.
perturbation.changed_text: Derivative Materials will be destroyed within 30 days of request by the Company and such destruction will be confirmed to the Company in writing by an officer of the Recipient.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'immediately destroyed' with 'destroyed within 30 days of request' introduces a delay in the destruction of Derivative Materials. This contradicts the overall theme of strict confidentiality and immediate action, implicitly creating a contradiction with Section 2.1 where 'all Confidential Information will be kept confidential.' The 30-day window creates a period where the confidential information in the Derivative Materials is potentially at risk, which conflicts with the intent of keeping the Confidential Information confidential.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 2.1 Non-Disclosure and Non-Use Covenants . As a condition to the Company’s disclosure of the Confidential Information to the Recipient, the Recipient hereby covenants and agrees that all Confidential Information will be kept confidential by the Recipient and will not, without the prior written consent of the Company, be disclosed by the Recipient, in any manner whatsoever, in whole or in part, and will not be used by the Recipient, directly or indirectly, for any purpose other than facilitating discussion with the Company. Moreover, the Recipient agrees to transmit the Confidential Information to only those Representatives who need to know the Confidential Information for the purpose of facilitating the Recipient’s discussion with the Company and who are informed of the confidential nature of the Confidential Information and the terms of this Agreement. The Recipient hereby acknowledges that it is aware, and the Recipient agrees that it will advise its Representatives who are informed as to the matters which are the subject of this Agreement, that (i) the Confidential Information being furnished may contain or may itself be material, non-public information concerning the Company, and (ii) securities laws in the United States prohibit any person who has received material, non-public information concerning the Company or the matters which are the subject of this Agreement from purchasing or selling securities of the Company or from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person is likely to purchase or sell such securities. The Recipient agrees to not engage, directly or indirectly, in transactions in Company securities while in possession of Confidential 2 Information, and further agrees that is will be responsible for its compliance with federal and state securities laws. The Recipient will be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by the Recipient or by its Representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a 30-day window for the destruction of Derivative Materials, which directly conflicts with Section 2.1, which mandates that 'all Confidential Information will be kept confidential'. The delay creates a period where the confidentiality of the information is at risk, breaking the explicit confidentiality obligation.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient acknowledges that neither the Company nor any of its Representatives makes any representation or warranty as to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information. The Recipient agrees that neither the Company nor any of its Representatives shall have any liability to the Recipient as a result of their reliance on the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient acknowledges that the Company will use reasonable efforts to ensure the accuracy and completeness of Confidential Information provided, but the Company nor any of its Representatives makes any explicit representation or warranty as to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information. The Recipient agrees that neither the Company nor any of its Representatives shall have any liability to the Recipient as a direct result of their gross negligence in providing inaccurate Confidential Information, and the burden of proof lies on the recipient to make such claims and provide substantial evidence .
perturbation.explanation: The added text regarding 'reasonable efforts' and liability for 'gross negligence' introduces a level of responsibility on the Company that was previously disclaimed in Section 2.5. Section 2.5 originally stated that the company makes no warranty as to accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information. The addition opens the company up to make reasonable efforts, which introduces contradiction with the earlier statement within the section. This creates an in-text contradiction, potentially impacting the disclaimer's enforceability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 2.5 Disclaimers . The Recipient acknowledges that the Company will use reasonable efforts to ensure the accuracy and completeness of Confidential Information provided, but the Company nor any of its Representatives makes any explicit representation or warranty as to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information. The Recipient agrees that neither the Company nor any of its Representatives shall have any liability to the Recipient as a direct result of their gross negligence in providing inaccurate Confidential Information, and the burden of proof lies on the recipient to make such claims and provide substantial evidence . The Recipient hereby agrees that in no event will the Recipient have or assert any claims whatsoever against the Company or any of the Company’s Representatives relating to or in any way connected with the subject matter of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Company makes no representation or warranty as to the accuracy of the Confidential Information, and has no liability for reliance on it. The changed text introduces 'reasonable efforts' to ensure accuracy and accepts liability for 'gross negligence'. This directly contradicts the original disclaimer and the unchanged text which states that the Recipient acknowledges that the Company will use reasonable efforts to ensure the accuracy and completeness of Confidential Information provided, but the Company nor any of its Representatives makes any explicit representation or warranty as to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information. The Recipient agrees that neither the Company nor any of its Representatives shall have any liability to the Recipient as a direct result of their gross negligence in providing inaccurate Confidential Information, and the burden of proof lies on the recipient to make such claims and provide substantial evidence . The Recipient hereby agrees that in no event will the Recipient have or assert any claims whatsoever against the Company or any of the Company’s Representatives relating to or in any way connected with the subject matter of this Agreement. . This creates an in-text contradiction and aligns with the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: ARTICLE IV TERM AND TERMINATION Section 4.1 Term . Except as set forth in Article II, this Agreement shall terminate upon the earlier of (i) the Company’s 2012 Annual Meeting of Shareholders or (ii) the Company’s receipt of the notice required under the Company’s bylaws from JAM or its affiliates required to nominate a director for election at a meeting of the Company’s shareholders.
perturbation.changed_text: ARTICLE IV TERM AND TERMINATION Section 4.1 Term . This agreement shall remain in effect for one year from the date first written above, automatically renewing for successive one-year periods, unless earlier terminated pursuant to article 2. Except as set forth in Article II, this Agreement shall terminate upon the earlier of (i) the Company’s 2012 Annual Meeting of Shareholders or (ii) the Company’s receipt of the notice required under the Company’s bylaws from JAM or its affiliates required to nominate a director for election at a meeting of the Company’s shareholders.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of "This agreement shall remain in effect for one year from the date first written above, automatically renewing for successive one-year periods, unless earlier terminated pursuant to article 2" contradicts the original termination clause. The original clause specifies termination events related to the 2012 Annual Meeting and shareholder nomination. The added sentence introduces automatic renewal and broadens potential termination based on Article II, creating a potential in-text contradiction in Article 4, because the end date of the agreement is no longer precise.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 4.1 Term . This agreement shall remain in effect for one year from the date first written above, automatically renewing for successive one-year periods, unless earlier terminated pursuant to article 2. Except as set forth in Article II, this Agreement shall terminate upon the earlier of (i) the Company’s 2012 Annual Meeting of Shareholders or (ii) the Company’s receipt of the notice required under the Company’s bylaws from JAM or its affiliates required to nominate a director for election at a meeting of the Company’s shareholders.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text specified termination events related to the 2012 Annual Meeting and shareholder nomination. The added sentence, which now begins the section, introduces automatic renewal for successive one-year periods, contradicting the original termination clause based on the 2012 Annual Meeting and shareholder nomination. This creates a strong in-text contradiction in Article 4, because the end date of the agreement is no longer as precise as it was in the original contract, leading to ambiguity. This adheres to the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction. The Contradicted Text is the same as the Changed Text, but that doesn't mean there isn't a contradiction.
